data.title: Starbucks Turnaround Gathers Momentum as Analyst Lifts Price Target and Reiterates Buy Rating
data.description:
Wells Fargo analyst Zachary Fadem maintained a Buy rating on Starbucks yesterday and set a price target of $115.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Zachary Fadem has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Starbucks’ accelerating operating momentum and improved outlook. The company delivered stronger-than-expected comparable sales in North America, with traffic-led growth supported by service enhancements, product innovation, and loyalty initiatives, while global same-store sales also improved despite a challenging macro backdrop.

Fadem also highlights that cost headwinds from commodities and labor are easing sequentially, setting the stage for margin recovery as coffee prices moderate in the second half. In addition, management raised its fiscal 2026 guidance for comps and earnings, remodel programs and store operations are driving better throughput, and the China joint venture structure is now clearer and expected to be earnings-neutral, all supporting his view that the turnaround is gaining traction and justifying a higher price target and Buy rating.

Fadem covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Domino’s Pizza, AutoZone, and Best Buy Co. According to TipRanks, Fadem has an average return of 4.4% and a 53.68% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $116.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: CoStar Earns Buy Rating on Profit Upside and 2026 EBITDA Guidance Raise Despite Softer Bookings
data.description:
William Blair analyst Stephen Sheldon has maintained their bullish stance on CSGP stock, giving a Buy rating on April 27.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Stephen Sheldon has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including solid execution and continued profit upside despite softer bookings trends. CoStar delivered first-quarter revenue in line with expectations and materially exceeded profit forecasts, leading management to lift adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2026 while reaffirming its long-term margin and earnings targets.

In addition, Sheldon highlights healthy growth across key platforms, with LoopNet posting strong double-digit revenue gains and Apartments.com maintaining solid, if slightly slower, expansion. He also points to growing traction in important international markets and believes that prior negativity around bookings was largely reflected in the share price, creating an attractive risk-reward profile even after slightly trimming longer-term organic growth assumptions.

In another report released on April 27, KBW also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $57.00 price target.

data.title: Bernie McTernan Raises Rush Street Interactive Price Target to $33 on Stronger EBITDA Outlook and iGaming Growth
data.description:
Rush Street Interactive, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Bernie McTernan from Needham reiterated a Buy rating on the stock and has a $33.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bernie McTernan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including stronger forward estimates and ongoing operational outperformance at Rush Street Interactive. He raised his 2026 and 2027 adjusted EBITDA projections meaningfully and lifted his price target to $33, reflecting growing confidence in earnings power, supported by the company’s emphasis on iGaming and a more favorable tax backdrop in Colombia.

McTernan also highlights robust underlying trends, such as record first‑quarter depositors and better customer retention, which underscore the strength of the core franchise. Looking ahead, he points to multiple potential catalysts, including incremental demand from the upcoming World Cup, the planned launch in Alberta, and the possibility of securing an iGaming license in Maine, which could provide additional upside even after the stock’s strong performance over the past year.

According to TipRanks, McTernan is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 1.1% and a 42.57% success rate. McTernan covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as DraftKings, PENN Entertainment, and Chewy.

In another report released yesterday, Jefferies also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $32.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on TransUnion, Sees Undervalued Upside with Unchanged $95 Price Target
data.description:
In a report released today, Kyle Peterson from Needham reiterated a Buy rating on TransUnion, with a price target of $95.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kyle Peterson has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including TransUnion’s first-quarter performance surpassing market expectations on both revenue and earnings, driven in part by solid trends in mortgage and several international regions. He also notes that management only modestly raised guidance for acquired businesses while leaving core growth assumptions unchanged, reflecting caution around macro and geopolitical risks.

Peterson further argues that if economic volatility recedes and VantageScore secures even limited adoption in the mortgage channel, current forecasts could prove conservative. Given that the stock trades at roughly 13x FY27 earnings, below both its own history and comparable companies, he believes the valuation does not fully reflect these potential upsides, supporting his reiterated Buy rating and unchanged $95 price target.

In another report released yesterday, William Blair also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $70.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Corbus Pharmaceuticals Initiated at Buy on CRB-701 Head and Neck Cancer Opportunity, Undervalued Pipeline, and $45 Price Target
data.description:
Corbus Pharmaceuticals (CRBP) has received a new Buy rating, initiated by Guggenheim analyst, Paul Jeng.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Paul Jeng has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Corbus Pharmaceuticals’ lead asset and valuation. He views CRB-701 as a next‑generation Nectin‑4–targeting ADC with a meaningfully improved safety and tolerability profile versus Padcev, supported by both China and US/EU clinical data. These data show encouraging response rates in late‑line head and neck cancer that are above current standards of care and competitive agents, suggesting the potential for deeper and more durable responses.

Jeng also highlights Corbus’s strategy to concentrate CRB-701’s development on HPV‑positive oropharyngeal head and neck cancer, a sizable segment with high Nectin‑4 expression, limited EGFR‑targeted competition, and substantial unmet need. He believes this focused approach, combined with upcoming clinical catalysts that could clarify the regulatory path, is not fully reflected in the current market capitalization, which implies essentially no enterprise value. In addition, he sees the obesity candidate CRB‑913 as a higher‑risk but significant upside option, reinforcing an attractive risk/reward skew that supports his $45 price target and Buy rating.

CRBP’s price has also changed dramatically for the past six months – from $17.120 to $9.820, which is a -42.64% drop .
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
